chunk_map.heading: H1/lede: Compound and close the loop
Write your rules file from the session
Push back where the summary misreads
Reach past the repo with a connector
Wire one connector into your tracker
Send the close-out note to the ticket
Read the close-out and ship it
Sweep the session into your rules file one more time
Close the session, the loop is closed

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Referent resolution under a sequential deck read.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Candidate flags considered and dismissed: chunk 2 line 37 "the compound step" and chunk 3/4 lines 42/54 "the loop" both resolve to chunk 0 — the H1 is literally "Compound and *close the loop*" and the lede at line 9 names "the compound step (plan → work → review → compound, Kieran Klaassen's loop)". Both are chunk-0 lede/title nouns reused later, the explicit carve-out in the compendium's rule 1, not a violation.
Both links to mcp-and-connectors.md (chunks 3 and 4) name the doc by title in the link text and unpack the claim in the surrounding sentence — navigational-pointer carve-out applies.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every backward reference points at a beat that exists.
Live-demo beats survive a text read.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No guess-before-reveal beat in this file.

rules_evaluated.evidence: chunk 4 "Wire one connector into your tracker": line 49: "3. Manual paste: Claude writes the close-out; you paste it into the tracker UI yourself. Five seconds, no tool setup." — one of the chunk's three explicit paths wires no connector at all; line 50 itself concedes this ("For paths 1 or 2, get your connector up and running"), so the header's "Wire one connector" promise only covers 2 of the 3 listed options.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Loosen the header to cover all three routes (e.g. "Get one path into your tracker working") or scope the header explicitly to paths 1-2 and let path 3 sit under the following chunk's framing.
Checked all six sub-items (Not-X-Not-Y-Z, anaphora triples, self-grading superlatives, number+retraction, abstraction-noun stacks, empty-leg verb triplets) across all chunks; no hits.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Internal dialect never lands on a slide.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: chunk 4 line 54 "One connector firing on one ticket" uses "firing" in the plain-English trigger/succeed sense (an engine or synapse firing), not the compendium's rule-fires repo-jargon sense — reads clean on a wall.
